deck: Part two of Dimagi's values mini-series. Lucina Tse, Gillian Javetski, Avni Singhal, and Simon Kelly join hosts Jonathan Jackson and Amie Vaccaro to unpack what it means to lead with empathy and respect across a fast-growing, global, remote team.
seo_description: Dimagi's team unpacks its second core value, lead with empathy and respect, and what caring how the work gets done looks like across a global team.
og_description: Dimagi's team on its second core value: leading with empathy and respect across a global, remote team.

pull_quote.text: 80% of the time, it's easy to be respectful and empathetic. But that last 20% of the time, that's where the rubber really meets the road.

bullets: The value itself: treating each other with kindness and respect, creating space for all voices and stories to be heard
Why people stay: Avni Singhal on a flat organization where leaders patiently listen, and recognition comes without pressure to compete
Teams as people, not org charts: Simon Kelly on managing groups of individuals and standing in the gap during family challenges
Equity by design: Lucina Tse on asking whether every new benefit and process works fairly across Africa, India, the US, and beyond
Money where your mouth is: Gillian Javetski on Dimagi's transparent pay scale and balancing social-impact salaries with real recognition
The last 20%: leading with empathy under severe stress, and extending that respect to partners and the broader ecosystem, not just inside the team
Remote and cross-cultural: why in-person summits and human connection make empathy easier when communication gets hard

stats.label: of the time it is easy to be respectful and empathetic, per Jonathan Jackson
the harder stretch where leading with empathy and respect truly matters
the stressful travel some trips take to reach a destination

guests.name: Lucina Tse
guests.role: Chief Operating Officer, focused on equity, balance, and people operations across Dimagi's global regions.

guests.name: Gillian Javetski
guests.role: Chief of Staff, reflecting on Dimagi's transparent pay scale and long-standing commitment to equity.

guests.name: Avni Singhal
guests.role: Senior Customer Success Manager and member of the values council, sharing why the culture drew her to Dimagi.

guests.name: Simon Kelly
guests.role: Director of Server Engineering and member of the values council, on treating teams as groups of individuals.
